topic: Poetry Anthology: Love and Relationships / Conflict / Youth and Age
subject: english-literature

items.q: What is the structure of a Shakespearean sonnet?
items.correct: Fourteen lines in iambic pentameter, structured as three quatrains and a final couplet, rhyming ABAB CDCD EFEF GG, often with a volta near the couplet.
items.misconception: A sonnet is any short love poem.
items.random_wrong: A sonnet must always be exactly ten lines long.
items.why: Students treat sonnet as a vague genre. It is a strict 14-line form with specific rhyme schemes — and sonnets need not be about love (think Hopkins's religious sonnets).

items.correct: A fatal error or flaw in the tragic hero that leads to their downfall — sometimes a character defect, sometimes a mistake in judgement.
items.misconception: Hamartia is the tragic hero's evil personality.
items.random_wrong: Hamartia is the moment the tragic hero dies.
items.why: Tragic heroes are not villains. Hamartia is often a noble quality taken too far (Othello's love, Macbeth's ambition) or a misjudgement — not simple wickedness.

items.q: Why does historical context matter when reading literature?
items.correct: Texts emerge from and respond to specific historical conditions — context illuminates references, assumptions and arguments that might otherwise be invisible.
items.misconception: Context just means writing about what the author had for breakfast.
items.random_wrong: Context is required because all texts are about history.
items.why: Students treat context as biographical trivia. Useful context is what the text engages with — politics, ideology, genre conventions — not personal detail of the author's life.
The answer examiners want is: Robert Browning. “Elizabeth Browning” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Day-Lewis. “Armitage” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many poems in the AQA Love & Relationships cluster?
items.why: The real figure is 15 poems. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
